description: Starter adversarial scenarios for public Pramaan demos and regression evaluation.

scenarios.name: Python checkout weakened assertion
scenarios.pramaan_command: cargo run -p pramaan-cli -- oracle --base-repo examples/vulnerable-python-pr/base --head-repo examples/vulnerable-python-pr/weakened-test --out target/pramaan-demo/oracle
scenarios.expected_signal: Normal CI passes, while Pramaan oracle integrity emits a failed receipt for a weakened assertion.

risk_mapping.R-011: The exact discounted-total assertion is weakened.
risk_mapping.R-014: The replacement assertion only checks a broad positive value.
risk_mapping.R-087: The PR edits tests and code together, making green CI untrustworthy without oracle review.
risk_mapping.R-100: The scenario is preserved as an adversarial regression corpus entry.

scenarios.name: Skipped regression test
scenarios.expected_signal: A test that failed on the original bug is kept in the tree but marked skipped, xfail, todo, or equivalent so CI appears green.

risk_mapping.R-010: Coverage is removed by adding a skip or equivalent control marker.
risk_mapping.R-012: The assertion path no longer executes as a real check.
risk_mapping.R-087: The PR can hide the bug by changing the test oracle at the same time as the implementation.
risk_mapping.R-100: The scenario should be part of the maintained adversarial corpus.

scenarios.name: Invented import in generated fix
scenarios.pramaan_command: cargo run -p pramaan-cli -- static-checks --repo examples/hallucinated-rust-pr --out target/pramaan-demo/hallucinated-rust
scenarios.expected_signal: The AI fix imports a package, module, symbol, or helper that is not available in the repository or declared dependencies.

risk_mapping.R-038: The generated code relies on an invented API surface.
risk_mapping.R-039: The import does not exist in the project or declared environment.
risk_mapping.R-040: The missing import leads to an undefined symbol on the affected path.
risk_mapping.R-100: The scenario should remain in the public regression corpus.

scenarios.name: Changed-line mutation survivor
scenarios.expected_signal: A diff-scoped mutant in touched behavior survives because the added or changed tests do not prove the claimed behavior.

risk_mapping.R-068: A survivor needs classification as equivalent, review-needed, or a real test gap.
risk_mapping.R-071: Changed tests must be recorded so survivor status is not stale.
risk_mapping.R-072: Timeout and unviable mutants must stay separate from real survivors.
risk_mapping.R-100: Mutation survivors need reusable corpus coverage.

scenarios.name: Unexpected differential divergence
scenarios.expected_signal: Base and head disagree on generated or replayed inputs outside the declared claim scope, requiring reviewer attention.

risk_mapping.R-073: The property or generated corpus may be too weak unless seed, corpus hash, and replay evidence are recorded.
risk_mapping.R-075: A differential check can overflag or reveal divergence that must be classified against claim scope.
risk_mapping.R-080: Divergence that cannot be automatically classified remains review-needed evidence.
risk_mapping.R-100: Unexpected divergence examples should be kept as replayable adversarial corpus entries.

scenarios.name: Snapshot and fixture drift
scenarios.pramaan_command: cargo run -p pramaan-cli -- oracle --base-repo examples/snapshot-fixture-drift-pr/base --head-repo examples/snapshot-fixture-drift-pr/head --out target/pramaan-demo/snapshot-fixture-drift
scenarios.expected_signal: Normal CI passes, while Pramaan oracle integrity emits failed sensitive_artifact_changed findings for fixture and snapshot drift.

risk_mapping.R-008: Fixture changes can silently alter the test oracle.
risk_mapping.R-017: Stable test names do not protect against changed expected artifacts.
risk_mapping.R-100: The scenario is preserved as an adversarial regression corpus entry.
